category|Category:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:松尾神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11529679}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Matsuo Shrine |Image = [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|280px]]
Main hall (standing inside a protective shelter) |Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Asahicho, Imatoge 4 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|42.17|N|135|34|12.02|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Matsuo Shrine}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}) |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternate name = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Matsuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Matsuo Shrine''' (まつのおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Asahicho, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined Deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (おおやまぐいのみこと) * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (いちきしまひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, it is said to have been founded during the {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}), when {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|WD=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} enshrined the deities by order of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Matsuo Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have been revered as the tutelary shrine of nearby villages, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), many shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Tanba campaign of {{ill|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|Q313320|WD=Q313320|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}, with only the main hall escaping destruction. === Shrine Grounds === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otaki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Otaki]] The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Mount Saburōgatake on the eastern side of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}, and have been selected as one of the “100 Natural Sites of Kameoka.”[http://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/kankyouhozen/kurashi/kurashi/shizen/100sen/083.html 100 Natural Sites of Kameoka: Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka City website)]. The main hall was constructed in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Meiō era|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1498|Q6705|WD=Q6705|1=1498}}) during the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is a one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and currently stands inside a protective shelter. This main hall is registered as a cultural property by Kyoto Prefecture. In addition, the water used for purification located on the lower level beneath the main hall is spring water flowing from Mount Saburōgatake, known as “Otaki,” and is said never to have dried up even during severe droughts. === Auxiliary Shrines === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otabisho.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Otabisho]] '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Ōnamuchi Shrine (おおなむちじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Ebisu Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu-no-Kami|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Tenmangū Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * Susanoo Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Mizuhanome Shrine (みずはのめじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Takafusa Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} * Imamiya Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} '''Shrines outside the grounds''' * {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (Morimoto, Asahicho, Kameoka City, {{Coord|35|04|41.96|N|135|33|57.11|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otabisho}}) === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Matsuo Shrine Main Hall – Registered on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area === * Matsuo Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area – Designated on May 15, 1985. === On-site Information === '''Address''' * Asahicho Imatoge 4, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} '''Access''' * About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yagi Station|Q1043724|WD=Q1043724|1=Yagi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) === Notes === {{Commonscat|Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka)}} {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Matsuo Shrine}} == Categories == Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}) |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternate name = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Matsuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Matsuo Shrine''' (まつのおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Asahicho, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined Deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (おおやまぐいのみこと) * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (いちきしまひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, it is said to have been founded during the {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}), when {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|WD=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} enshrined the deities by order of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Matsuo Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have been revered as the tutelary shrine of nearby villages, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), many shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Tanba campaign of {{ill|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|Q313320|WD=Q313320|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}, with only the main hall escaping destruction. === Shrine Grounds === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otaki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Otaki]] The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Mount Saburōgatake on the eastern side of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}, and have been selected as one of the “100 Natural Sites of Kameoka.”[http://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/kankyouhozen/kurashi/kurashi/shizen/100sen/083.html 100 Natural Sites of Kameoka: Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka City website)]. The main hall was constructed in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Meiō era|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1498|Q6705|WD=Q6705|1=1498}}) during the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is a one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and currently stands inside a protective shelter. This main hall is registered as a cultural property by Kyoto Prefecture. In addition, the water used for purification located on the lower level beneath the main hall is spring water flowing from Mount Saburōgatake, known as “Otaki,” and is said never to have dried up even during severe droughts. === Auxiliary Shrines === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otabisho.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Otabisho]] '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Ōnamuchi Shrine (おおなむちじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Ebisu Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu-no-Kami|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Tenmangū Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * Susanoo Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Mizuhanome Shrine (みずはのめじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Takafusa Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} * Imamiya Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} '''Shrines outside the grounds''' * {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (Morimoto, Asahicho, Kameoka City, {{Coord|35|04|41.96|N|135|33|57.11|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otabisho}}) === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Matsuo Shrine Main Hall – Registered on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area === * Matsuo Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area – Designated on May 15, 1985. === On-site Information === '''Address''' * Asahicho Imatoge 4, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} '''Access''' * About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yagi Station|Q1043724|WD=Q1043724|1=Yagi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) === Notes === {{Commonscat|Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka)}} category|Category:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:松尾神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11529679}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Matsuo Shrine |Image = [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|280px]]
Main hall (standing inside a protective shelter) |Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Asahicho, Imatoge 4 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|42.17|N|135|34|12.02|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Matsuo Shrine}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}) |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternate name = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Matsuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Matsuo Shrine''' (まつのおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Asahicho, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined Deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (おおやまぐいのみこと) * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (いちきしまひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, it is said to have been founded during the {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}), when {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|WD=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} enshrined the deities by order of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Matsuo Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have been revered as the tutelary shrine of nearby villages, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), many shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Tanba campaign of {{ill|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|Q313320|WD=Q313320|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}, with only the main hall escaping destruction. === Shrine Grounds === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otaki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Otaki]] The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Mount Saburōgatake on the eastern side of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}, and have been selected as one of the “100 Natural Sites of Kameoka.”[http://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/kankyouhozen/kurashi/kurashi/shizen/100sen/083.html 100 Natural Sites of Kameoka: Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka City website)]. The main hall was constructed in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Meiō era|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1498|Q6705|WD=Q6705|1=1498}}) during the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is a one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and currently stands inside a protective shelter. This main hall is registered as a cultural property by Kyoto Prefecture. In addition, the water used for purification located on the lower level beneath the main hall is spring water flowing from Mount Saburōgatake, known as “Otaki,” and is said never to have dried up even during severe droughts. === Auxiliary Shrines === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otabisho.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Otabisho]] '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Ōnamuchi Shrine (おおなむちじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Ebisu Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu-no-Kami|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Tenmangū Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * Susanoo Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Mizuhanome Shrine (みずはのめじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Takafusa Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} * Imamiya Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} '''Shrines outside the grounds''' * {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (Morimoto, Asahicho, Kameoka City, {{Coord|35|04|41.96|N|135|33|57.11|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otabisho}}) === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Matsuo Shrine Main Hall – Registered on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area === * Matsuo Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area – Designated on May 15, 1985. === On-site Information === '''Address''' * Asahicho Imatoge 4, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} '''Access''' * About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yagi Station|Q1043724|WD=Q1043724|1=Yagi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) === Notes === {{Commonscat|Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka)}} {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}) |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternate name = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Matsuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Matsuo Shrine''' (まつのおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Asahicho, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined Deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (おおやまぐいのみこと) * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (いちきしまひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, it is said to have been founded during the {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}), when {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|WD=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} enshrined the deities by order of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Matsuo Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have been revered as the tutelary shrine of nearby villages, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), many shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Tanba campaign of {{ill|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|Q313320|WD=Q313320|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}, with only the main hall escaping destruction. === Shrine Grounds === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otaki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Otaki]] The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Mount Saburōgatake on the eastern side of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}, and have been selected as one of the “100 Natural Sites of Kameoka.”[http://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/kankyouhozen/kurashi/kurashi/shizen/100sen/083.html 100 Natural Sites of Kameoka: Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka City website)]. The main hall was constructed in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Meiō era|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1498|Q6705|WD=Q6705|1=1498}}) during the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is a one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and currently stands inside a protective shelter. This main hall is registered as a cultural property by Kyoto Prefecture. In addition, the water used for purification located on the lower level beneath the main hall is spring water flowing from Mount Saburōgatake, known as “Otaki,” and is said never to have dried up even during severe droughts. === Auxiliary Shrines === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otabisho.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Otabisho]] '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Ōnamuchi Shrine (おおなむちじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Ebisu Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu-no-Kami|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Tenmangū Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * Susanoo Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Mizuhanome Shrine (みずはのめじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Takafusa Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} * Imamiya Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} '''Shrines outside the grounds''' * {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (Morimoto, Asahicho, Kameoka City, {{Coord|35|04|41.96|N|135|33|57.11|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otabisho}}) === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Matsuo Shrine Main Hall – Registered on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area === * Matsuo Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area – Designated on May 15, 1985. === On-site Information === '''Address''' * Asahicho Imatoge 4, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} '''Access''' * About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yagi Station|Q1043724|WD=Q1043724|1=Yagi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) === Notes === {{Commonscat|Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}) |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternate name = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Matsuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Matsuo Shrine''' (まつのおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Asahicho, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined Deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (おおやまぐいのみこと) * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (いちきしまひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, it is said to have been founded during the {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}), when {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|WD=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} enshrined the deities by order of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Matsuo Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have been revered as the tutelary shrine of nearby villages, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), many shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Tanba campaign of {{ill|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|Q313320|WD=Q313320|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}, with only the main hall escaping destruction. === Shrine Grounds === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otaki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Otaki]] The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Mount Saburōgatake on the eastern side of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}, and have been selected as one of the “100 Natural Sites of Kameoka.”[http://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/kankyouhozen/kurashi/kurashi/shizen/100sen/083.html 100 Natural Sites of Kameoka: Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka City website)]. The main hall was constructed in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Meiō era|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1498|Q6705|WD=Q6705|1=1498}}) during the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is a one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and currently stands inside a protective shelter. This main hall is registered as a cultural property by Kyoto Prefecture. In addition, the water used for purification located on the lower level beneath the main hall is spring water flowing from Mount Saburōgatake, known as “Otaki,” and is said never to have dried up even during severe droughts. === Auxiliary Shrines === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otabisho.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Otabisho]] '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Ōnamuchi Shrine (おおなむちじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Ebisu Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu-no-Kami|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Tenmangū Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * Susanoo Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Mizuhanome Shrine (みずはのめじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Takafusa Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} * Imamiya Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} '''Shrines outside the grounds''' * {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (Morimoto, Asahicho, Kameoka City, {{Coord|35|04|41.96|N|135|33|57.11|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otabisho}}) === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Matsuo Shrine Main Hall – Registered on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area === * Matsuo Shrine Cultural Property Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) === Notes === {{Commonscat|Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka)}} It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined Deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (おおやまぐいのみこと) * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (いちきしまひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, it is said to have been founded during the {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}), when {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|WD=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} enshrined the deities by order of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Matsuo Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have been revered as the tutelary shrine of nearby villages, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), many shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Tanba campaign of {{ill|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|Q313320|WD=Q313320|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}, with only the main hall escaping destruction. === Shrine Grounds === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otaki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Otaki]] The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Mount Saburōgatake on the eastern side of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}, and have been selected as one of the “100 Natural Sites of Kameoka.”[http://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/kankyouhozen/kurashi/kurashi/shizen/100sen/083.html 100 Natural Sites of Kameoka: Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka City website)]. The main hall was constructed in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Meiō era|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1498|Q6705|WD=Q6705|1=1498}}) during the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is a one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and currently stands inside a protective shelter. This main hall is registered as a cultural property by Kyoto Prefecture. In addition, the water used for purification located on the lower level beneath the main hall is spring water flowing from Mount Saburōgatake, known as “Otaki,” and is said never to have dried up even during severe droughts. === Auxiliary Shrines === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otabisho.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Otabisho]] '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Ōnamuchi Shrine (おおなむちじんじゃ) – Enshrined category|Category:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:松尾神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11529679}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Matsuo Shrine |Image = [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|280px]]
Main hall (standing inside a protective shelter) |Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Asahicho, Imatoge 4 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|42.17|N|135|34|12.02|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Matsuo Shrine}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}) |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternate name = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Matsuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Matsuo Shrine''' (まつのおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Asahicho, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined Deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (おおやまぐいのみこと) * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (いちきしまひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, it is said to have been founded during the {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}), when {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|WD=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} enshrined the deities by order of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Matsuo Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have been revered as the tutelary shrine of nearby villages, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), many shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Tanba campaign of {{ill|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|Q313320|WD=Q313320|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}, with only the main hall escaping destruction. === Shrine Grounds === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otaki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Otaki]] The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Mount Saburōgatake on the eastern side of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}, and have been selected as one of the “100 Natural Sites of Kameoka.”[http://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/kankyouhozen/kurashi/kurashi/shizen/100sen/083.html 100 Natural Sites of Kameoka: Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka City website)]. The main hall was constructed in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Meiō era|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1498|Q6705|WD=Q6705|1=1498}}) during the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is a one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and currently stands inside a protective shelter. This main hall is registered as a cultural property by Kyoto Prefecture. In addition, the water used for purification located on the lower level beneath the main hall is spring water flowing from Mount Saburōgatake, known as “Otaki,” and is said never to have dried up even during severe droughts. === Auxiliary Shrines === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otabisho.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Otabisho]] '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Ōnamuchi Shrine (おおなむちじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Ebisu Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu-no-Kami|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Tenmangū Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * Susanoo Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Mizuhanome Shrine (みずはのめじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Takafusa Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} * Imamiya Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} '''Shrines outside the grounds''' * {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (Morimoto, Asahicho, Kameoka City, {{Coord|35|04|41.96|N|135|33|57.11|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otabisho}}) === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Matsuo Shrine Main Hall – Registered on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area === * Matsuo Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area – Designated on May 15, 1985. === On-site Information === '''Address''' * Asahicho Imatoge 4, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} '''Access''' * About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yagi Station|Q1043724|WD=Q1043724|1=Yagi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) === Notes === {{Commonscat|Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka)}} {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined Deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (おおやまぐいのみこと) * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (いちきしまひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, it is said to have been founded during the {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}), when {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|WD=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} enshrined the deities by order of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Matsuo Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have been revered as the tutelary shrine of nearby villages, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), many shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Tanba campaign of {{ill|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|Q313320|WD=Q313320|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}, with only the main hall escaping destruction. === Shrine Grounds === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otaki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Otaki]] The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Mount Saburōgatake on the eastern side of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}, and have been selected as one of the “100 Natural Sites of Kameoka.”[http://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/kankyouhozen/kurashi/kurashi/shizen/100sen/083.html 100 Natural Sites of Kameoka: Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka City website)]. The main hall was constructed in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Meiō era|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1498|Q6705|WD=Q6705|1=1498}}) during the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is a one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and currently stands inside a protective shelter. This main hall is registered as a cultural property by Kyoto Prefecture. In addition, the water used for purification located on the lower level beneath the main hall is spring water flowing from Mount Saburōgatake, known as “Otaki,” and is said never to have dried up even during severe droughts. === Auxiliary Shrines === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otabisho.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Otabisho]] '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Ōnamuchi Shrine (おおなむちじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Ebisu Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu-no-Kami|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Tenmangū Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * Susanoo Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Mizuhanome Shrine (みずはのめじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Takafusa Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} * Imamiya Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} '''Shrines outside the grounds''' * {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (Morimoto, Asahicho, Kameoka City, {{Coord|35|04|41.96|N|135|33|57.11|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otabisho}}) === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Matsuo Shrine Main Hall – Registered on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area === * Matsuo Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area – Designated on May 15, 1985. === On-site Information === '''Address''' * Asahicho Imatoge 4, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} '''Access''' * About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yagi Station|Q1043724|WD=Q1043724|1=Yagi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) === Notes === {{Commonscat|Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka)}} category|Category:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:松尾神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11529679}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Matsuo Shrine |Image = [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|280px]]
Main hall (standing inside a protective shelter) |Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Asahicho, Imatoge 4 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|42.17|N|135|34|12.02|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Matsuo Shrine}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}) |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternate name = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Matsuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Matsuo Shrine''' (まつのおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Asahicho, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined Deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (おおやまぐいのみこと) * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (いちきしまひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, it is said to have been founded during the {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}), when {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|WD=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} enshrined the deities by order of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Matsuo Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have been revered as the tutelary shrine of nearby villages, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), many shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Tanba campaign of {{ill|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|Q313320|WD=Q313320|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}, with only the main hall escaping destruction. === Shrine Grounds === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otaki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Otaki]] The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Mount Saburōgatake on the eastern side of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}, and have been selected as one of the “100 Natural Sites of Kameoka.”[http://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/kankyouhozen/kurashi/kurashi/shizen/100sen/083.html 100 Natural Sites of Kameoka: Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka City website)]. The main hall was constructed in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Meiō era|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1498|Q6705|WD=Q6705|1=1498}}) during the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is a one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and currently stands inside a protective shelter. This main hall is registered as a cultural property by Kyoto Prefecture. In addition, the water used for purification located on the lower level beneath the main hall is spring water flowing from Mount Saburōgatake, known as “Otaki,” and is said never to have dried up even during severe droughts. === Auxiliary Shrines === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otabisho.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Otabisho]] '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Ōnamuchi Shrine (おおなむちじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Ebisu Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu-no-Kami|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Tenmangū Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * Susanoo Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Mizuhanome Shrine (みずはのめじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Takafusa Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} * Imamiya Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} '''Shrines outside the grounds''' * {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (Morimoto, Asahicho, Kameoka City, {{Coord|35|04|41.96|N|135|33|57.11|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otabisho}}) === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Matsuo Shrine Main Hall – Registered on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area === * Matsuo Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area – Designated on May 15, 1985. === On-site Information === '''Address''' * Asahicho Imatoge 4, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} '''Access''' * About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yagi Station|Q1043724|WD=Q1043724|1=Yagi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) === Notes === {{Commonscat|Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka)}} {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}) |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternate name = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Matsuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Matsuo Shrine''' (まつのおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Asahicho, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined Deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (おおやまぐいのみこと) * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (いちきしまひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, it is said to have been founded during the {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}), when {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|WD=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} enshrined the deities by order of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Matsuo Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have been revered as the tutelary shrine of nearby villages, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), many shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Tanba campaign of {{ill|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|Q313320|WD=Q313320|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}, with only the main hall escaping destruction. === Shrine Grounds === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otaki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Otaki]] The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Mount Saburōgatake on the eastern side of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}, and have been selected as one of the “100 Natural Sites of Kameoka.”[http://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/kankyouhozen/kurashi/kurashi/shizen/100sen/083.html 100 Natural Sites of Kameoka: Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka City website)]. The main hall was constructed in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Meiō era|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1498|Q6705|WD=Q6705|1=1498}}) during the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is a one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and currently stands inside a protective shelter. This main hall is registered as a cultural property by Kyoto Prefecture. In addition, the water used for purification located on the lower level beneath the main hall is spring water flowing from Mount Saburōgatake, known as “Otaki,” and is said never to have dried up even during severe droughts. === Auxiliary Shrines === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otabisho.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Otabisho]] '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Ōnamuchi Shrine (おおなむちじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Ebisu Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu-no-Kami|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Tenmangū Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * Susanoo Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Mizuhanome Shrine (みずはのめじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Takafusa Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} * Imamiya Shrine – Enshrined deity: It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined Deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (おおやまぐいのみこと) * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (いちきしまひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, it is said to have been founded during the {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}), when {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|WD=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} enshrined the deities by order of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Matsuo Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have been revered as the tutelary shrine of nearby villages, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), many shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Tanba campaign of {{ill|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|Q313320|WD=Q313320|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}, with only the main hall escaping destruction. === Shrine Grounds === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otaki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Otaki]] The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Mount Saburōgatake on the eastern side of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}, and have been selected as one of the “100 Natural Sites of Kameoka.”[http://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/kankyouhozen/kurashi/kurashi/shizen/100sen/083.html 100 Natural Sites of Kameoka: Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka City website)]. The main hall was constructed in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Meiō era|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1498|Q6705|WD=Q6705|1=1498}}) during the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is a one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and currently stands inside a protective shelter. This main hall is registered as a cultural property by Kyoto Prefecture. In addition, the water used for purification located on the lower level beneath the main hall is spring water flowing from Mount Saburōgatake, known as “Otaki,” and is said never to have dried up even during severe droughts. === Auxiliary Shrines === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otabisho.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Otabisho]] '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Ōnamuchi Shrine (おおなむちじんじゃ) – Enshrined category|Category:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:松尾神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11529679}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Matsuo Shrine |Image = [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|280px]]
Main hall (standing inside a protective shelter) |Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Asahicho, Imatoge 4 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|42.17|N|135|34|12.02|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Matsuo Shrine}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}) |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternate name = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Matsuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Matsuo Shrine''' (まつのおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Asahicho, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined Deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (おおやまぐいのみこと) * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (いちきしまひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, it is said to have been founded during the {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|WD=Q23528|1=715}}), when {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|WD=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} enshrined the deities by order of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Matsuo Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said to have been revered as the tutelary shrine of nearby villages, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), many shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Tanba campaign of {{ill|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|Q313320|WD=Q313320|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}, with only the main hall escaping destruction. === Shrine Grounds === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otaki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Otaki]] The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Mount Saburōgatake on the eastern side of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}, and have been selected as one of the “100 Natural Sites of Kameoka.”[http://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/kankyouhozen/kurashi/kurashi/shizen/100sen/083.html 100 Natural Sites of Kameoka: Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka City website)]. The main hall was constructed in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Meiō era|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1498|Q6705|WD=Q6705|1=1498}}) during the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is a one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and currently stands inside a protective shelter. This main hall is registered as a cultural property by Kyoto Prefecture. In addition, the water used for purification located on the lower level beneath the main hall is spring water flowing from Mount Saburōgatake, known as “Otaki,” and is said never to have dried up even during severe droughts. === Auxiliary Shrines === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otabisho.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Otabisho]] '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Ōnamuchi Shrine (おおなむちじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Ebisu Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu-no-Kami|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Tenmangū Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Hachiman Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * Susanoo Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Mizuhanome Shrine (みずはのめじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Takafusa Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} * Imamiya Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} '''Shrines outside the grounds''' * {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (Morimoto, Asahicho, Kameoka City, {{Coord|35|04|41.96|N|135|33|57.11|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otabisho}}) === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Matsuo Shrine Main Hall – Registered on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area === * Matsuo Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area – Designated on May 15, 1985. === On-site Information === '''Address''' * Asahicho Imatoge 4, {{ill|lt=Kameoka City|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} '''Access''' * About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yagi Station|Q1043724|WD=Q1043724|1=Yagi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) === Notes === {{Commonscat|Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka)}} {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Matsuo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined Deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamagui no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (おおやまぐいのみこと) * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (いちきしまひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine tradition, it is said to have been founded during the {{ill|lt=Wadō era|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|qid=Q23511|1=708}}–{{ill|lt=715|Q23528|qid=Q23528|1=715}}), when {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|qid=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} enshrined the deities by order of {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|qid=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Matsuo Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|qid=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|qid=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. It is said to have been revered as the tutelary shrine of nearby villages, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|qid=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|qid=Q6769|1=1592}}), many shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Tanba campaign of {{ill|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|Q313320|qid=Q313320|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}, with only the main hall escaping destruction. === Shrine Grounds === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otaki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Otaki]] The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Mount Saburōgatake on the eastern side of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|qid=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}, and have been selected as one of the “100 Natural Sites of Kameoka.”[http://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/kankyouhozen/kurashi/kurashi/shizen/100sen/083.html 100 Natural Sites of Kameoka: Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka City website)]. The main hall was constructed in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Meiō era|Q1193841|qid=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1498|Q6705|qid=Q6705|1=1498}}) during the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is a one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof, and currently stands inside a protective shelter. This main hall is registered as a cultural property by Kyoto Prefecture. In addition, the water used for purification located on the lower level beneath the main hall is spring water flowing from Mount Saburōgatake, known as “Otaki,” and is said never to have dried up even during severe droughts. === Auxiliary Shrines === [[File:Matsuo-jinja (Kameoka) otabisho.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Otabisho]] '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Ōnamuchi Shrine (おおなむちじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Ebisu Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|qid=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu-no-Kami|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Ōjin}} * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * Susanoo Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Mizuhanome Shrine (みずはのめじんじゃ) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Takafusa Shrine
